jokes.text: Mistrial! Mistrial! Yes, yes, I missed your trial!
jokes.setup: Dusty shouting 'Mistrial!' in court
jokes.punchline: He mishears it as 'I missed your trial!' and responds accordingly
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific joke showing Dusty's inexperience and confusion in legal settings. Clean wordplay with immediate payoff.

jokes.text: Well, had I known that she was talking about Lindsay, I would have been, like, 'Goodbye, Felicia.'
jokes.setup: Buster explaining he thought Lucille 2 was talking about him being her child
jokes.punchline: Using modern slang 'Goodbye, Felicia' completely out of character
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character contradiction - Buster using contemporary slang is hilariously unexpected and perfectly timed.

jokes.text: I did, but nothing's gonna happen to me 'cause I'm just a boy.
jokes.setup: Michael asking if Buster pushed Mimi down stairs
jokes.punchline: Buster's childlike defense despite being an adult man
jokes.explanation: Classic Buster character comedy - his arrested development makes this both funny and sad.

jokes.text: Lottie. She's so mean. I wish I could shove her down the st... Hey! Shh!
jokes.setup: Discussing how Lottie will make Buster look like someone who pushes old ladies
jokes.punchline: Buster immediately starts saying he wants to push Lottie down stairs
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing - Buster proving the prosecution's point immediately after being warned about it.

jokes.text: No touching! Or touching. Yeah, feel it. It's a rental.
jokes.setup: Michael saying 'No touching!' then Dusty touching something
jokes.punchline: Dusty's proud admission it's a rental
jokes.explanation: Good callback combined with Dusty's character trait of being proud of small achievements.

jokes.text: body-shaped thing... It's from a magician.
jokes.setup: Footage shows Buster dumping what looks like a body
jokes.punchline: Gob's casual euphemism for what's clearly meant to look like a corpse
jokes.explanation: Gob's typical casual approach to clearly suspicious activity.

jokes.text: He's gonna have to stay gay. Gob assumed he'd been ghosted.
jokes.setup: Gob thinking Tony Wonder disappeared
jokes.punchline: Using modern dating term 'ghosted' in context of magician literally disappearing
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting modern dating terminology with magic tricks. Economical and well-crafted.

jokes.text: We know you're as straight as an Oklahoma highway.
jokes.setup: Gay Mafia threatening Gob about faking being gay
jokes.punchline: Very specific regional reference to notoriously straight highways
jokes.explanation: Unexpected specific geographical reference makes this threat both menacing and absurd.

jokes.text: Then we'll let you age out.
jokes.setup: Gob asking if he has to stay gay for seven years
jokes.punchline: Treating sexual orientation like a job with retirement benefits
jokes.explanation: Absurd bureaucratization of sexual identity with darkly comic undertones.

jokes.text: Mm-hmm. Yeah, I saw A Few Good Men, too. But Dusty's the real deal. Sorry, Michael. Game recognizes game.
jokes.setup: Michael using dramatic language from legal movies
jokes.punchline: Buster calling out the reference while using his own cliché
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about movie references while Buster uses his own sports cliché. Good character moment.

jokes.text: She asked you to get rid of family memories? Why? Is that weird? It's just that, typically, the drinking does that.
jokes.setup: Dusty asking if it's weird Lucille wants to destroy family tapes
jokes.punchline: Michael's dark joke about alcoholism erasing memories
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly structured joke about family dysfunction. Michael's dry delivery makes it land.

jokes.text: Mother, I'm going to open the box! Mom's not here, so it turns out she's not as all-powerful as I think she is.
jokes.setup: Dusty being told not to open the box by Lucille
jokes.punchline: His childlike rebellion and realization
jokes.explanation: Shows how being called 'Mother' has infantilized Dusty. His rebellion is hilariously small-scale.

jokes.text: I know which one I would watch with a gun to my head. Not the hi-res.
jokes.setup: Choice between watching George's affairs in different resolutions
jokes.punchline: Michael preferring lower quality to see less of his father's infidelity
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery and relatable family trauma humor. The beat and misdirection work perfectly.

jokes.text: Awesome. '82 was awesome. Good year.
jokes.setup: Michael realizing 1982 was when traumatic family event happened
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt to change subject
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Michael's discomfort with family trauma. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: These are three-quarter-inch, Michael. This is not a TV station in the '70s.
jokes.setup: Michael asking why Dusty hasn't watched the tapes
jokes.punchline: Technical complaint about outdated video format
jokes.explanation: Decent technical humor. Shows Dusty's inexperience while being somewhat relatable.

jokes.text: But, fortunately, Michael knew someone at a news station in his 70s.
jokes.setup: Need to play old video format
jokes.punchline: John Beard being in his 70s vs. having '70s equipment
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with perfect economy. Narrator's dry delivery makes the misdirection work well.

jokes.text: Could you really enjoy your freedom, knowing that you hurt my career?
jokes.setup: Gob trying to convince Buster to lie for him
jokes.punchline: Making it about his magic career rather than murder charges
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob narcissism - making Buster's murder trial about his own inconvenience.

jokes.text: And violate magician-assistant privilege? Not an option!
jokes.setup: Buster suggesting they could lie about the dummy
jokes.punchline: Gob treating magic ethics as seriously as attorney-client privilege
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor. Gob's misplaced priorities and fake professional ethics are perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I don't know. I'll use Adhir. But if you are acquitted, we can start as soon as next week, 'cause I got a big illusion planned.
jokes.setup: Buster asking what happens if he's convicted
jokes.punchline: Gob casually having a backup assistant and already planning the next show
jokes.explanation: Shows Gob's complete lack of concern for Buster's fate while maintaining his show business priorities.

jokes.text: They were two tough guys right out of Guys and Dolls, a play they were in the middle of a very successful four-week run of at a local community theater.
jokes.setup: Menacing Gay Mafia members making threats
jokes.punchline: Revealing they're just community theater actors
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that recontextualizes their over-the-top performance. Makes their threats both funny and still somewhat real.

jokes.text: Reminds me of how Hanks walked around like Jim Lovell for a year.
jokes.setup: Discussion of actors staying in character
jokes.punchline: Reference to Tom Hanks method acting for Apollo 13
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that reinforces they're actors while making an amusing Hollywood reference.

jokes.text: Well, cis male, high butch. Gob had learned a lot in the short time he'd been magically gay.
jokes.setup: Gob describing his transformation back to straight
jokes.punchline: Using very specific LGBTQ+ terminology he's recently learned
jokes.explanation: Funny character development showing Gob awkwardly integrating new vocabulary into his act.

jokes.text: Well, John, you used our house for To Entrap a Local Predator. That's it. You were one of our super creeps.
jokes.setup: John recognizing Michael from somewhere
jokes.punchline: Michael was caught in a predator sting at his own house
jokes.explanation: Great callback that adds new embarrassing context to Michael's past actions.

jokes.text: Objection. Is this going somewhere? Yeah, that's what I wanted to find out.
jokes.setup: Prosecutor objecting to Dusty's questioning
jokes.punchline: Dusty admitting he doesn't know where he's going with it
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Dusty's inexperience. His honesty is both charming and incompetent.

jokes.text: I'll allow it. I won't allow that. That's... Sustained. That's... That's fine. That's fair.
jokes.setup: Confusing legal back-and-forth
jokes.punchline: Judge getting increasingly confused about what to allow
jokes.explanation: Good escalating confusion showing how the trial is falling apart due to Dusty's incompetence.

jokes.text: You know, he just... he was just so good at acting like, 'Where'd she go? What happened to her? She just... She vanished.'
jokes.setup: Gob praising Buster as his best assistant
jokes.punchline: Describing skills that sound exactly like covering up a murder
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor where Gob unknowingly describes murder skills while praising magic assistance. Perfect irony.

jokes.text: Doctor-to-be, Tobias Fünke. Subject, who is now in a deep hypnotic state, and this is my doctoral research material on memory suppression.
jokes.setup: Finding old videotapes
jokes.punchline: Tobias doing pseudo-psychology research while not actually being a doctor
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Tobias's delusions of professional competence while setting up the hypnosis reveal.

jokes.text: Give him some juice, please.
jokes.setup: Hypnotherapy session with young Buster
jokes.punchline: Child asking for juice during traumatic memory implantation session
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy of child's innocent needs during psychological manipulation.

jokes.text: Juice, please! What really happened is your mother pushed Mimi down the stairs. But what do we tell the police? Mother pushed Mimi down the stairs. No, no, no, no. Please remove the juice, please!
jokes.setup: Hypnosis session trying to implant false memories
jokes.punchline: Complete chaos with Buster getting confused about what lie to tell
jokes.explanation: Chaotic but well-structured scene showing family dysfunction. The juice obsession adds absurdist element.

jokes.text: I did it, but nothing's gonna happen to me 'cause I'm just a boy. Can you say it again? ...but nothing's gonna happen to me 'cause I'm a boy. I wish I hadn't finished that juice.
jokes.setup: Lucille making Buster repeat the false confession
jokes.punchline: Buster's random comment about the juice in this traumatic moment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor mixing child abuse with Buster's innocent juice fixation. The quotable line becomes tragically funny.

jokes.text: A Few Good Men. Great movie. But first, I got to wash the pile... Sure. Take your time.
jokes.setup: Michael saying 'if Mother can handle the truth'
jokes.punchline: John recognizing another movie reference and Michael's awkward exit
jokes.explanation: Simple meta-joke about movie references becoming obvious. Decent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Interesting, huh? Hers were a lot easier than his.
jokes.setup: Lottie finishing her cross-examination of Gob
jokes.punchline: Casual comment about how easy it was to destroy the defense
jokes.explanation: Good professional smugness from Lottie, showing her competence vs. Dusty's bumbling.

jokes.text: Oh, that'll be great timing, because I've been drinking this free court water. Does the prosecution have any objections? I need a break. Oh, let him go. Oh, he worked through recess.
jokes.setup: Michael asking to join the defense
jokes.punchline: Everyone immediately needing bathroom breaks
jokes.explanation: Decent escalating joke about everyone's human needs taking precedence over the trial.

jokes.text: I think you mean 'the accused's mother.' Oh, what did I say?
jokes.setup: Michael saying 'accused mother'
jokes.punchline: Judge correcting him while Michael doesn't realize his Freudian slip
jokes.explanation: Clever foreshadowing that works as both a slip-up and hint about Lucille's guilt. Economical and layered.

jokes.text: Look at you, milking this for every moment. Isn't this classic Michael? We make fun of him all the time. Right, Gob?
jokes.setup: Michael trying to conduct serious legal examination
jokes.punchline: Lucille treating it like family gossip time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille behavior - undermining Michael's authority even during a murder trial.

jokes.text: are you going for one of your Few Good Men moments?
jokes.setup: Michael building to dramatic courtroom revelation
jokes.punchline: Lucille recognizing and dismissing Michael's theatrical tendencies
jokes.explanation: Meta joke where character recognizes the show's own pattern of movie references.

jokes.text: Oh, God, are you going for one of your Few Good Men moments?
jokes.setup: Michael building to dramatic courtroom moment
jokes.punchline: Lucille calling out his movie-inspired tactics
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about legal drama tropes while staying in character for Lucille's dismissiveness.

jokes.text: You're goddamn right I did!
jokes.setup: Michael's interrogation of Lucille escalating out of control
jokes.punchline: Michael accidentally confessing to murder using classic movie dialogue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to running movie reference gag where Michael accidentally convicts himself using iconic dialogue.

jokes.text: You're goddamn right I did! And that's how Michael A Few Good Men-ed himself.
jokes.setup: Michael's dramatic courtroom confrontation
jokes.punchline: He accidentally confesses instead of getting confession from Lucille
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to the movie references. Michael's incompetence creates perfect ironic reversal of the famous scene.

jokes.text: Nice humblebrag.
jokes.setup: Michael confessing to offering to sleep with Lucille 2 for debt forgiveness
jokes.punchline: Lucille calling it a humblebrag
jokes.explanation: Perfect use of modern terminology by Lucille to deflate Michael's dramatic confession.

jokes.text: It was a stunningly dramatic twist, a real Making of a Monster-type moment.
jokes.setup: Michael's dramatic confession in court
jokes.punchline: Narrator comparing it to true crime documentaries
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on the show's own dramatic style while referencing popular documentary formats.

jokes.text: Two brothers can't be charged for the same crime. Well, I hate to say it, but you may have been getting better advice from Barry.
jokes.setup: Dusty explaining why Michael won't be charged
jokes.punchline: Michael realizing Barry Zuckerkorn might actually be better
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how low the bar is set when even Barry might be preferable to current situation.

jokes.text: Never say that! Gob is the worst. Yeah. Yeah. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Michael saying he's the worst Bluth
jokes.punchline: Lucille immediately redirecting to Gob
jokes.explanation: Perfect mother-son moment showing Lucille's priorities and family dynamics.

jokes.text: People with liberal-tardation.
jokes.setup: Lucille calling protesters 'libtards'
jokes.punchline: Lucille's offensive portmanteau delivered with complete confidence
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate offensive language that's more shocking than clever.

jokes.text: Oh, the protesters. I don't know who let these libtards in here. I beg your pardon? People with liberal-tardation.
jokes.setup: Sally asking about protesters
jokes.punchline: Lucille's offensive made-up term and doubling down
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate offensive humor that shows Lucille's casual cruelty while making Sally uncomfortable.

jokes.text: What do we want? No wall! When do we want it? We don't? That doesn't... Go limp! Go limp! Go... No, no, no, no. You... You look like you're at a Walking Dead convention. No, limp, like, uh, flaccid. Unerect.
jokes.setup: Protesters doing chants
jokes.punchline: Completely confused protest strategy and Tobias's explicit clarification
jokes.explanation: Good escalating chaos with Tobias's inappropriate but earnest instruction clarification.

jokes.text: Three dollars an hour and a copy of the news footage for my reel.
jokes.setup: Someone asking what Tobias is being paid to protest
jokes.punchline: Absurdly low payment plus footage for acting portfolio
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment showing his desperation for any performing opportunity.

jokes.text: I wear the horns of a cuckold. That's why you don't put your girlfriend in the same sleeping bag with your son. But I'm not gonna let him share mine. I like to spread my legs. Apparently, so does DeBrie.
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining his relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about sleeping arrangements and sexual habits
jokes.explanation: Peak Tobias oversharing with perfect inappropriate detail. Character-specific cringe humor.

jokes.text: Today, in these plywood forms, 10,000 pounds of wet, gay, QuickHard cement will be poured, because if we build it, they won't come!
jokes.setup: Gob presenting the wall demonstration
jokes.punchline: Describing cement as 'gay' and butchering Field of Dreams quote
jokes.explanation: Absurdist Gob logic combining his gay experience with construction materials and movie references.

jokes.text: How does it feel to be accused of murder? It was a lot of work, but I didn't do it! They found me not guilty. Well, it was a mistrial, so they found you not not guilty.
jokes.setup: Reporter asking about murder trial
jokes.punchline: Buster's confusion about legal outcomes and double negative
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay about legal technicalities combined with Buster's childlike understanding.

jokes.text: We have a word for this in Chinese. It means a combination of embarrassment and obligation. We need a word like that.
jokes.setup: Watching Gob's embarrassing performance
jokes.punchline: George Michael wishing English had that specific word
jokes.explanation: Clever linguistic observation that perfectly captures the family dynamic situation.

jokes.text: Howard you like me now, Dad? Howard you like me now?
jokes.setup: George Michael angry at Michael for opposing the sale
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'How about' and the Howard family name
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works both as pun and emotional expression of George Michael's frustration.

jokes.text: You could both use a hit. I'm sorry. As did Michael.
jokes.setup: George Michael comparing Michael to Ron Howard
jokes.punchline: Threatening violence and narrator confirming Michael participated
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation showing family relationships breaking down into actual violence.

jokes.text: My God, they're such a bunch of drama thugs. Well, I might not have been dead, but my career was. I was doing three shows a day in Branson in the, uh, Yakov Smirnoff Theater. What's he like? Disappointing.
jokes.setup: Tony explaining his career troubles
jokes.punchline: Yakov Smirnoff being disappointing in person
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about meeting celebrities and career disappointments.

jokes.text: they're such a bunch of drama thugs.
jokes.setup: Tony explaining Gay Mafia's behavior
jokes.punchline: Dismissing threatening criminals as theatrical performers
jokes.explanation: Perfect oxymoron that captures the absurd theatricality of the Gay Mafia.

jokes.text: You're too chicken for that. You're the chicken, and not a spring one. How dare you say that to me! Chick-chick. Chick-chick-chick.
jokes.setup: Argument escalating between Michael and Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Michael doing literal chicken impressions
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation showing Michael's regression to childish behavior in the flashback.

jokes.text: According to the succeeding New York Times, Donald Trump doesn't even have a... a chance of winning that election. Oh, I don't know, don't count out his likability.
jokes.setup: Michael dismissing Trump's election chances
jokes.punchline: Lucille citing his 'likability' as a positive
jokes.explanation: Topical political humor with hindsight irony, plus Lucille's characteristic contrarian position.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Lucille 2 is your mother? Mimi. Not Lucille 2. Oh, right. That is who I pushed down the stairs.
jokes.setup: Confusion about which elderly woman Buster pushed
jokes.punchline: Buster casually confirming the murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection joke where the punchline is Buster's casual admission of actual murder.

jokes.text: Hey, gang, does Buster's mannequin look like... Judy Garland. Is it Lucille 2? Why is she smiling? This isn't a mannequin. This is... the happiest corpse I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the mannequin in the wall
jokes.punchline: Discovering it's actually Lucille 2's corpse and she looks happy
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy revelation that subverts expectations while being genuinely shocking and funny.

jokes.text: Okay, I did it. So... But doesn't she remind you of Mom?
jokes.setup: Buster finally admitting to the murder
jokes.punchline: Immediately focusing on how the corpse resembles his mother
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific ending that ties together all of Buster's psychological issues in one perfect line.

jokes.text: Okay, I did it. So...
jokes.setup: Family discovering Lucille 2's actual body in wall
jokes.punchline: Buster's casual confession to actual murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of season-long mystery with Buster's characteristically casual confession to murder.

jokes.text: But doesn't she remind you of Mom?
jokes.setup: Buster confessing to murder
jokes.punchline: Immediately deflecting to noting victim's resemblance to mother
jokes.explanation: Perfect final Buster moment showing his psychological motivation while maintaining his childlike detachment.
